\noindent The residual plots in Figure 5.9 are obtained by:

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Add Pearson residuals to the data
USSteamCoEstim$resid <- residuals(ussteamco.mod.2, type = "pearson")

# Extract predictor names
predictors <- all.vars(formula(ussteamco.mod.2))[-1]

# Function to create residual plot
make_resid_plot <- function(data, var) {
  n_unique <- length(unique(data[[var]]))

  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[var]], y = resid)) +
    geom_point(color = "#00338D", alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
    labs(x = var, y = "")

  if (n_unique > 2) {
    p <- p +
      geom_smooth(
        method = "lm",
        formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
        se = FALSE,
        color = "#E36877"
      )
  } else {
    p <- p +
      geom_smooth(
        method = "lm",
        formula = y ~ x,
        se = FALSE,
        color = "#E36877"
      )
  }

  p
}

# Create residual plots for each predictor
plots <- lapply(predictors, function(var) {
  make_resid_plot(USSteamCoEstim, var)
})

# Fitted values vs residuals plot
fitted_data <- data.frame(
  fitted = fitted(ussteamco.mod.2),
  resid = USSteamCoEstim$resid
)

fitted_plot <- ggplot(fitted_data, aes(x = fitted, y = resid)) +
  geom_point(color = "#00338D", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = FALSE,
    color = "#E36877"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(x = "Fitted Values", y = "")

# Top grid: 2x2 layout of the first 4 residual plots
top <- arrangeGrob(
  grobs = plots[1:4],
  ncol = 2
)

# Combine top and bottom
combined <- arrangeGrob(
  top,
  fitted_plot,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(2, 1)
)

# Display
grid.newpage()
grid.draw(combined)
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
